segments.text: And Elisha the prophet called one of the children of the prophets, and said unto him,
Gird up thy loins, and take this box of oil in thine hand, and go to Ramoth-gilead:
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: And when thou comest thither, look out there Jehu the son of Jehoshaphat the son of Nimshi, and go in, and make him arise up from among his brethren, and carry him to an inner chamber;

footnotes: 9.2 inner…: Heb. chamber in a chamber

segments.text: Then take the box of oil, and pour it on his head, and say,
Thus saith the LORD, I have anointed thee king over Israel.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Then open the door, and flee, and tarry not.
¶ So the young man, even the young man the prophet, went to Ramoth-gilead.
And when he came, behold, the captains of the host were sitting; and he said,
I have an errand to thee, O captain.
segments.speaker: Young man

segments.text: Unto which of all us?
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: To thee, O captain.
segments.speaker: Young man

segments.text: And he arose, and went into the house; and he poured the oil on his head, and said unto him,
Thus saith the LORD God of Israel, I have anointed thee king over the people of the LORD, even over Israel.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And thou shalt smite the house of Ahab thy master, that I may avenge the blood of my servants the prophets, and the blood of all the servants of the LORD, at the hand of Jezebel.
For the whole house of Ahab shall perish: and I will cut off from Ahab him that pisseth against the wall, and him that is shut up and left in Israel:
And I will make the house of Ahab like the house of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, and like the house of Baasha the son of Ahijah:
And the dogs shall eat Jezebel in the portion of Jezreel, and there shall be none to bury her.
And he opened the door, and fled.
¶ Then Jehu came forth to the servants of his lord: and one said unto him,
Is all well? wherefore came this mad fellow to thee?
segments.speaker: Servant

segments.text: Ye know the man, and his communication.
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: And they said, It is false; tell us now.
Thus and thus spake he to me, saying, Thus saith the LORD, I have anointed thee king over Israel.
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: Then they hasted, and took every man his garment, and put it under him on the top of the stairs, and blew with trumpets, saying,
Jehu is king.
segments.speaker: Crowd

segments.text: So Jehu the son of Jehoshaphat the son of Nimshi conspired against Joram.
(Now Joram had kept Ramoth-gilead, he and all Israel, because of Hazael king of Syria.)
But king Joram was returned to be healed in Jezreel of the wounds which the Syrians had given him, when he fought with Hazael king of Syria.
If it be your minds, then let none go forth nor escape out of the city to go to tell it in Jezreel.
segments.speaker: Jehu

footnotes: 9.15 Joram: Heb. Jehoram but not in verse sixteen; 9.15 had given: Heb. smote; 9.15 let none…: Heb. let no escaper go, etc

segments.text: So Jehu rode in a chariot, and went to Jezreel; for Joram lay there.
And Ahaziah king of Judah was come down to see Joram.
And there stood a watchman on the tower in Jezreel, and he spied the company of Jehu as he came, and said,
I see a company.
segments.speaker: Watchman

segments.text: Take an horseman, and send to meet them, and let him say, Is it peace?
segments.speaker: Joram

segments.text: So there went one on horseback to meet him, and said,
Thus saith the king, Is it peace?
segments.speaker: King

segments.text: What hast thou to do with peace? turn thee behind me.
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: Then he sent out a second on horseback, which came to them, and said,
Thus saith the king, Is it peace?
segments.speaker: King

segments.text: What hast thou to do with peace? turn thee behind me.
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: And the watchman told, saying, He came even unto them, and cometh not again: and the driving is like the driving of Jehu the son of Nimshi; for he driveth furiously.

footnotes: 9.20 driving: or, marching; 9.20 furiously: Heb. in madness

segments.text: And Joram said, Make ready.
Make ready.
segments.speaker: Joram

segments.text: And his chariot was made ready.
And Joram king of Israel and Ahaziah king of Judah went out, each in his chariot, and they went out against Jehu, and met him in the portion of Naboth the Jezreelite.

footnotes: 9.21 Make ready: Heb. Bind; 9.21 met: Heb. found

segments.text: And it came to pass, when Joram saw Jehu, that he said,
Is it peace, Jehu?
segments.speaker: Joram

segments.text: What peace, so long as the whoredoms of thy mother Jezebel and her witchcrafts are so many?
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: And Joram turned his hands, and fled, and said to Ahaziah,
There is treachery, O Ahaziah.
segments.speaker: Joram

segments.text: And Jehu drew a bow with his full strength, and smote Jehoram between his arms, and the arrow went out at his heart, and he sunk down in his chariot.

footnotes: 9.24 drew…: Heb. filled his hand with a bow; 9.24 sunk: Heb. bowed

segments.text: Then said Jehu to Bidkar his captain,
Take up, and cast him in the portion of the field of Naboth the Jezreelite: for remember how that, when I and thou rode together after Ahab his father, the LORD laid this burden upon him;
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: Surely I have seen yesterday the blood of Naboth, and the blood of his sons, saith the LORD;
and I will requite thee in this plat, saith the LORD.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Now therefore take and cast him into the plat of ground, according to the word of the LORD.

footnotes: 9.26 blood: Heb. bloods; 9.26 plat: or, portion

segments.text: ¶ But when Ahaziah the king of Judah saw this, he fled by the way of the garden house.
And Jehu followed after him, and said,
Smite him also in the chariot.
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: And they did so at the going up to Gur, which is by Ibleam.
And he fled to Megiddo, and died there.
And his servants carried him in a chariot to Jerusalem, and buried him in his sepulchre with his fathers in the city of David.
And in the eleventh year of Joram the son of Ahab began Ahaziah to reign over Judah.
¶ And when Jehu was come to Jezreel, Jezebel heard of it; and she painted her face, and tired her head, and looked out at a window.

footnotes: 9.30 painted…: Heb. put her eyes in painting

segments.text: And as Jehu entered in at the gate, she said,
Had Zimri peace, who slew his master?
segments.speaker: Jezebel

segments.text: And he lifted up his face to the window, and said,
Who is on my side? who?
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: And there looked out to him two or three eunuchs.
And he said,
Throw her down.
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: So they threw her down: and some of her blood was sprinkled on the wall, and on the horses: and he trode her under foot.
And when he was come in, he did eat and drink, and said,
Go, see now this cursed woman, and bury her: for she is a king’s daughter.
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: And they went to bury her: but they found no more of her than the skull, and the feet, and the palms of her hands.
Wherefore they came again, and told him.
This is the word of the LORD, which he spake by his servant Elijah the Tishbite, saying, In the portion of Jezreel shall dogs eat the flesh of Jezebel:
segments.speaker: Jehu

footnotes: 9.36 by: Heb. by the hand of

segments.text: And the carcase of Jezebel shall be as dung upon the face of the field in the portion of Jezreel; so that they shall not say, This is Jezebel.
